title: Hardcoded absolute paths reduce portability and break on other machines
description: Hardcoded absolute paths like 'C:\PowerShellGUI' create machine-specific dependencies that break when the project is cloned, moved, or run on another system. This violates portability principles and makes CI/CD impossible. Use $PSScriptRoot, $WorkspacePath parameters, or Join-Path with variables instead. Test files are the most common offenders.
detection_method: Regex scan for string literals containing absolute drive paths to the workspace
preventionRule: Never hardcode absolute paths. Use $PSScriptRoot for script-relative paths, $WorkspacePath parameters for functions, or resolve via Join-Path with environment variables.
remedy: Replace hardcoded paths with $PSScriptRoot, $WorkspacePath, or $script:WS variable. For test files, use BeforeAll { $script:WS = $PSScriptRoot | Split-Path } or a parameter.
